 {"id":"bd-1mdk","title":"EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)","description":"# EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)

## PURPOSE
Replace traditional B-tree extent indexes with Bw-Trees (Buzzword Trees) — lock-free delta-chain trees that allow concurrent readers and writers without any reader blocking. This is a long-term performance optimization for the extent index hot path.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7:
- Bw-Tree (Levandoski et al., ICDE 2013): latch-free B-tree with delta chains
- Key insight: instead of in-place mutation, append delta records to page
- Readers never block: they traverse the delta chain from newest to oldest
- Writers use CAS to append deltas (no locks)
- Periodic consolidation merges delta chains back into base pages

## DESIGN

### Architecture
- Mapping table: PageId -> AtomicPtr<PageDelta>
- Delta chain: linked list of delta records (insert, delete, split, merge)
- Base page: consolidated B-tree node
- Consolidation: background thread merges delta chains when they exceed threshold

### Delta Records
```rust
enum PageDelta {
    Insert { key: ExtentKey, value: ExtentValue, next: *const PageDelta },
    Delete { key: ExtentKey, next: *const PageDelta },
    Split { split_key: ExtentKey, new_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Merge { removed_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Base(BTreeNode),
}
```

### Concurrency
- Lookup: traverse mapping table -> delta chain -> base page (all read-only, no locks)
- Insert/Delete: create delta, CAS into mapping table (retry on conflict)
- Split/Merge: structural modification via delta + CAS
- Consolidation: create new base page, CAS to replace delta chain

### Extent Index Integration
- Replace extent B-tree in ffs-ext4 and ffs-btrfs with Bw-Tree
- Same lookup API: find_extent(inode, logical_block) -> PhysicalExtent
- Same mutation API: insert_extent, delete_extent, split_extent

## CURRENT PRIORITY
P2 — performance optimization, not blocking correctness. Current B-tree works correctly.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Lock-free lookup: readers never block on writers
- [ ] CAS-based insert/delete: no mutex on hot path
- [ ] Delta chain consolidation prevents unbounded chain growth
- [ ] Benchmark: lookup throughput under concurrent writers vs B-tree with locks
- [ ] Correctness: all B-tree invariants maintained (sorted, balanced, split/merge correct)
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for current extent index

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E benchmark (bd-1mdk.4) shows improvement over locked B-tree
3. Unit tests (bd-1mdk.3) pass
4. Delta chain consolidation compacts correctly
5. Mapping table is lock-free (no mutex in hot path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:46.207227957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541669980Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541602964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","index","lockfree"],"comments":[{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-1mdk.1","title":"Implement Bw-Tree mapping table and delta chain core","description":"# Implement Bw-Tree mapping table and delta chain core

## GOAL
Implement the foundational data structures for a Bw-Tree (Levandoski et al., ICDE 2013): the mapping table (PageId -> AtomicPtr<PageDelta>) and delta chain (linked list of insert/delete/split/merge deltas appended via CAS).

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7: Bw-Trees are latch-free B-trees that replace in-place mutation with append-only delta records. Readers traverse the delta chain from newest to oldest, never blocking. Writers use compare-and-swap (CAS) to atomically append deltas. This eliminates all reader-side synchronization.

## DESIGN

### Mapping Table
```rust
struct MappingTable {
    pages: Vec<AtomicPtr<PageDelta>>,
    next_page_id: AtomicU64,
}
```
- Fixed-size array of atomic pointers (one per page/node)
- All B-tree operations go through mapping table indirection
- Enables lock-free structural modifications (split/merge update a pointer, not a tree)

### Delta Chain
```rust
enum PageDelta {
    Insert { key: ExtentKey, value: ExtentValue, next: *const PageDelta },
    Delete { key: ExtentKey, next: *const PageDelta },
    Split { separator: ExtentKey, new_sibling: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Merge { removed_sibling: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Base(BTreeNode),
}
```
- Each delta is a small record prepended to the chain via CAS
- Lookup: walk chain applying deltas to reconstruct current state
- Base page: consolidated snapshot of all deltas (periodic compaction target)

### CAS Append Protocol
1. Read current head pointer from mapping table
2. Create new delta with next = current head
3. CAS mapping_table[page_id] from current to new delta
4. On CAS failure: retry (another writer appended concurrently)

### Memory Management
- Delta nodes allocated via crossbeam-epoch (same EBR as MVCC versions)
- Old deltas freed after consolidation and epoch advance
- No manual memory management or unsafe deallocation

## RUST IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Use `crossbeam::epoch::{Atomic, Owned, Shared, Guard}` for all pointers
- `unsafe` blocks required for Shared pointer dereference (justified by epoch pin safety)
- #[repr(C)] for delta variants to ensure stable layout
- Consider cache-line alignment for mapping table entries to avoid false sharing

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] MappingTable supports allocate_page, get_page, cas_page operations
- [ ] Delta chain supports Insert, Delete append via CAS
- [ ] Lookup correctly traverses delta chain to find current value
- [ ] CAS retry loop handles concurrent append conflicts
- [ ] crossbeam-epoch used for all delta allocation and reclamation
- [ ] Unit tests: single-threaded insert/delete/lookup round-trip
- [ ] Unit tests: concurrent CAS append (2+ threads, verify no lost updates)
- [ ] Unit tests: delta chain traversal returns correct merged state
- [ ] Logging: tracing spans for CAS attempts, retries, and chain length at lookup time","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:01.435985132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:19.704819462Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:19.704794174Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","core","lockfree"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:01.435985132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":81,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:18Z"},{"id":165,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-1mdk.2","title":"Implement Bw-Tree consolidation (delta chain compaction)","description":"# Implement Bw-Tree consolidation (delta chain compaction)

## GOAL
Implement background consolidation that merges accumulated delta records back into a base page, preventing unbounded delta chain growth and maintaining lookup performance.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7: Without consolidation, delta chains grow linearly with writes, degrading lookup from O(log n) to O(log n + chain_length). Consolidation creates a fresh base page incorporating all deltas, then atomically replaces the chain via CAS on the mapping table.

## DESIGN

### Consolidation Trigger
- Chain length threshold: consolidate when delta chain exceeds configurable max (default: 16 deltas)
- Background thread: periodic scan of all pages for chains exceeding threshold
- Opportunistic: reader that encounters long chain can trigger inline consolidation

### Consolidation Protocol
1. Pin epoch (prevent GC of chain during consolidation)
2. Traverse delta chain, collecting all Insert/Delete operations
3. Apply operations to base page, producing new consolidated base
4. CAS mapping_table[page_id] from old chain head to new base
5. On CAS success: defer_destroy old chain nodes
6. On CAS failure: discard new base, retry (new deltas appended concurrently)

### Split/Merge During Consolidation
- If consolidated page exceeds max size: split into two pages
- If consolidated page below min size and sibling below threshold: merge
- Split/Merge are themselves delta records, enabling concurrent structural modifications

### Structural Modification Protocol (SMO)
- Split: create new sibling page, append Split delta to original, append Insert delta to parent
- Merge: append Merge delta to removed page, append Delete delta to parent
- All via CAS — no locks, no latches

## RUST IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Consolidation can race with concurrent inserts: CAS handles this correctly
- crossbeam-epoch Guard must be held during entire consolidation
- New base page: Owned::new(PageDelta::Base(consolidated_node))
- Location: ffs-bwtree/src/consolidation.rs

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Consolidation merges delta chain into fresh base page
- [ ] CAS atomically replaces old chain with consolidated base
- [ ] Concurrent writers during consolidation handled (CAS retry)
- [ ] Chain length bounded by consolidation threshold
- [ ] Split triggered when consolidated page too large
- [ ] Merge triggered when consolidated page too small
- [ ] Unit tests: consolidation produces correct base page
- [ ] Unit tests: concurrent consolidation + insert race
- [ ] Benchmark: lookup latency before/after consolidation
- [ ] Logging: tracing spans for consolidation trigger, duration, chain length before/after","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:19.279073429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:05:38.214945707Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:05:38.214923155Z","close_reason":"Implemented Bw-Tree delta chain consolidation in bw_tree.rs: consolidate_page (materialize + CAS replace), chain_length, scan_for_consolidation, consolidate_all, ConsolidationConfig/ConsolidationResult types. Fixed pre-existing clippy issues (use_self, significant_drop_tightening). 13 new tests: chain_length basics, consolidation reduces chain to 1, data preservation across inserts/overwrites/deletes/splits/merges, skip-if-already-base, scan finds long chains, consolidate_all selective, concurrent consolidation+inserts, result field correctness, idempotent re-consolidation, inserts-after-consolidation, stress interleaved (4 pages x 4 inserters + 4 consolidators). All 33 ffs-btree tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","compaction"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:19.279073429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:19.279073429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:18Z"},{"id":164,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

@@ -286,7 +286,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
-| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects, and mounted-path ext4 plus btrfs `os.pwritev(..., RWF_HIPRI)` coverage now proves accepted calls preserve caller-offset overwrite semantics. `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID` is likewise kept as a metadata write hint despite sharing raw bit 2 with `RWF_SYNC`; unit coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without an accidental full fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file; the Linux-invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination rejects with `EINVAL` before EOF lookup or mutation. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, invalid append/NOAPPEND conflicts, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_HIPRI`, `RWF_APPEND`, `RWF_NOAPPEND`, the invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` pair, and unsupported `RWF_ATOMIC`/`RWF_DONTCACHE` intents, proving sync writes become immediately readable and survive remount, and that harmless hints, append/NOAPPEND offset semantics, and unsupported-intent no-mutation contracts are exercised on both ext4 and btrfs when the host permits FUSE and forwards the flags. |
+| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects, and mounted-path ext4 plus btrfs `os.pwritev(..., RWF_HIPRI)` coverage now proves accepted calls preserve caller-offset overwrite semantics. `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID` is likewise kept as a metadata write hint despite sharing raw bit 2 with `RWF_SYNC`; unit coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without an accidental full fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file; the Linux-invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination rejects with `EINVAL` before EOF lookup or mutation. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, invalid append/NOAPPEND conflicts, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_HIPRI`, `RWF_APPEND`, `RWF_NOAPPEND`, the invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` pair, and unsupported `RWF_ATOMIC`/`RWF_DONTCACHE` intents, proving sync writes become immediately readable and survive remount on both ext4 and btrfs, and that harmless hints, append/NOAPPEND offset semantics, and unsupported-intent no-mutation contracts are exercised on both formats when the host permits FUSE and forwards the flags. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
